title: Identify/secure oncology expert + patient-advocate reviewers (for high-risk layer)
context: Any patient-facing plain-language summary (task plainlang-019) is classified riskTier:high and requires mandatory credentialed oncologist sign-off AND patient-advocate sign-off before publication. No high-risk output may ship until these reviewers are secured. This outreach task identifies and confirms both roles in M0 so they are ready before any high-risk task starts.
objective: Identify and confirm at least one credentialed oncologist reviewer and at least one patient advocate who agree to review patient-facing summaries before publication, and record their commitment in a committed document.
acceptanceCriteria: At least one credentialed oncologist (MD or equivalent with oncology expertise) is identified and confirms willingness to review patient-facing plain-language summaries before publication.
At least one patient advocate (with cancer-patient community affiliation or relevant lived experience) is identified and confirms willingness to review patient-facing summaries before publication.
Both reviewers understand their role: confirming 'not medical advice' framing, clinical accuracy of plain-language content, and prominence of research-use-only disclaimers.
Names, affiliations, and contact methods for both reviewers are recorded in a committed document.
Document notes that no patient-facing (riskTier:high) output may ship without both sign-offs recorded in the delivering task's acceptanceCriteria.
resources: PLAN.md — Quality, review & risk gates; Governance, roles & stakeholders
output: A committed document recording the confirmed oncology expert and patient-advocate reviewers and their agreement to the high-risk review role.
